name: Kokand
description: Kokand is one of the Fergana Valley’s oldest major cities and was capital of the Kokand Khanate (1709–1876). A centre of culture and crafts with 300+ mosques and monuments; population over 314,000; hosts an international applied arts festival. History from 10th c. as Khavakend/Khokand; 18th c. khanate under Shahrukh Bey. On the lower Sokh River; wood carving, pottery and silk traditions.

monuments.title: Khudayar Khan Palace
monuments.info: 1871 palace of the last Kokand khan — seven courtyards, 119 rooms; now regional museum; famous portal, tiles and ganch carving.

monuments.title: Jome Mosque
monuments.info: 1819–22 by Khan Umar; long iwan on ~100 carved columns — a masterpiece of Central Asian woodwork; 98 columns, 22.5 m minaret.

monuments.title: Norbutabiy Madrasa
monuments.info: c. 1795–98, largest madrasa of Kokand; also called Madrasai Mir; fine fired-brick façade.

monuments.title: Modari Khan Mausoleum
monuments.info: 1825 for Khan Umar’s mother, linked to poetess Nodira; turquoise dome and tile portal near the royal necropolis.

monuments.title: Dasturkhanchi Madrasa
monuments.info: 1833 by vizier Mahmud Dasturkhanchi; 25 cells, classroom and mosque; 68 students; restored for visitors.
